# Barcelona city guide

## Overview

Barcelona is where Gothic architecture meets Mediterranean beach culture. Gaudi's surreal buildings dot the skyline, tapas bars spill onto every sidewalk, and the energy doesn't start until 10 PM. It's creative, laid-back, sun-soaked, and has a Catalan identity that's fiercely proud and distinctly not "just Spanish."

**Best time to visit**: May-Jun and Sep-Oct for warm weather without peak summer crowds. Aug is when locals leave and tourists arrive.
**Currency**: Euro (EUR). 1 EUR ≈ 1.08 USD.
**Language tip**: Catalan is the primary local language, not Spanish. "Gracies" (thank you in Catalan) earns respect. Spanish works everywhere too.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Gothic Quarter (Barri Gotic) | Medieval, atmospheric | History, tapas, narrow streets, Cathedral | Metro L4, Jaume I |
| El Born | Trendy, cultural | Picasso Museum, cocktail bars, boutiques | Metro L4, Jaume I |
| La Barceloneta | Beach, casual | Seafood, sunbathing, boardwalk walks | Metro L4, Barceloneta |
| Eixample | Grand, Modernist | Gaudi buildings, upscale dining, shopping | Metro L3/L4, Passeig de Gracia |
| El Raval | Multicultural, gritty | Street art, MACBA, diverse food, nightlife | Metro L3, Liceu |
| Gracia | Village, bohemian | Local squares, vermouth bars, Parc Guell | Metro L3, Fontana |
| Poble Sec | Local, foodie | Pintxos bars, Montjuic access, live music | Metro L3, Poble Sec |
| Sant Antoni | Up-and-coming | Brunch, markets, craft beer | Metro L2, Sant Antoni |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| La Boqueria Market | Market | La Rambla | $-$$ | Barcelona's most famous food market. Fresh juices, jamon, seafood, and fruit at vibrant stalls. Go early to avoid crowds. | Fresh fruit smoothie, jamon iberico, seafood cone |
| Cal Pep | Tapas/seafood | El Born | $$-$$$ | Counter-service tapas from a legendary chef. The fried fish and tortilla are perfection. Book ahead or queue. | Fried fish, tortilla espanola, clams |
| Cerveceria Catalana | Tapas | Eixample | $$ | One of the best tapas bars in Barcelona. Packed at every meal. The montaditos are miniature masterpieces. | Patatas bravas, montaditos, grilled prawns |
| Can Paixano (La Xampanyeria) | Wine/tapas | La Barceloneta | $ | Standing-room cava bar with cheap sparkling wine and cured meat sandwiches. Loud, packed, and joyful. | Cava rosado, jamon bocadillo |
| Quimet y Quimet | Tapas | Poble Sec | $$ | Tiny standing-only bar famous for creative montaditos (small open sandwiches). Four generations of excellence. | Salmon yogurt honey montadito, smoked sardines |
| La Pepita | Tapas | Gracia | $$ | Modern tapas in a fun, graffiti-covered space. Creative small plates and excellent vermouth. | Pepita burger, truffle croquettes |
| El Xampanyet | Wine bar | El Born | $ | Traditional cava bar with blue-tiled walls. Anchovies, olives, and cheap cava. Old Barcelona at its best. | House cava, anchovies, olives |
| Tickets | Creative tapas | Parallel | $$$ | Albert Adria's playful take on tapas. Molecular gastronomy meets Spanish tradition. Book way ahead. | Liquid olive, air baguette, foie gras cotton candy |
| Bar Celta Pulperia | Galician | El Raval | $$ | Galician-style octopus bar. Pulpo a la gallega (paprika octopus) that melts in your mouth. | Pulpo a la gallega, pimientos de padron |
| Flax and Kale | Healthy | Eixample | $$ | Flexitarian restaurant with creative plant-forward dishes. Beautiful interior, great brunch. | Acai bowl, truffle pizza, cold-pressed juice |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| La Sagrada Familia | Architecture | Eixample | $$$ | 2-3 hours | Gaudi's unfinished masterpiece. Book tickets months ahead with a tower visit. The interior light through stained glass is breathtaking. |
| Park Guell | Architecture/Nature | Gracia | $$ | 1-2 hours | Gaudi's mosaic-covered park overlooking the city. The monumental zone requires timed tickets. Go early morning. |
| Gothic Quarter Walk | Walk | Barri Gotic | Free | 2-3 hours | Wander medieval streets past Roman ruins, the Cathedral, and hidden plazas. Get lost on purpose. |
| La Barceloneta Beach | Beach | La Barceloneta | Free | 2-4 hours | Barcelona's main beach. Swim, sunbathe, eat paella, play volleyball. Busy but fun. |
| Casa Batllo | Architecture | Eixample | $$$ | 1-1.5 hours | Gaudi's bone-house with an immersive night experience. The rooftop dragon spine is iconic. |
| Picasso Museum (Museu Picasso) | Art | El Born | $$ | 2 hours | Early Picasso works in five medieval palaces. The Las Meninas series is remarkable. Free first Sunday of month. |
| Montjuic Castle and Gardens | Views/Nature | Montjuic | $-$$ | 3-4 hours | Take the cable car up for panoramic views. Explore the castle, gardens, and MNAC museum. |
| La Rambla | Walk | Central | Free | 1 hour | The famous boulevard. Touristy but worth one walk. Watch for pickpockets. Better for people-watching than shopping. |
| Camp Nou (FC Barcelona) | Sports | Les Corts | $$ | 2 hours | One of the world's largest football stadiums. Stadium tour includes museum, pitch, and changing rooms. |
| Bunkers del Carmel | Views | El Carmel | Free | 1-2 hours | Civil War bunkers with the best free panoramic view in Barcelona. Bring wine, watch sunset with locals. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Carrer de Joaquin Costa | Bars | El Raval | $-$$ | The street that never sleeps. Dive bars, craft cocktails, and international crowds spilling onto the sidewalk. |
| Razzmatazz | Club | Poblenou | $$ | Five rooms, five music styles. Barcelona's biggest club. Concerts early, club night later. |
| Paradiso | Speakeasy | El Born | $$-$$$ | Enter through a pastrami shop refrigerator. Ranked among the world's best bars. Creative cocktails, small space. |
| Sala Apolo | Live music/club | Poble Sec | $-$$ | Historic music venue that becomes a club after midnight. Nasty Mondays is legendary. |
| La Terrazza | Outdoor club | Montjuic | $$ | Open-air summer club inside Poble Espanyol. Dancing under the stars, open May to October. |

## Transit tips

- **T-Casual card**: 10-trip card valid on Metro, bus, tram, and FGC trains. Much cheaper than single tickets.
- **Metro**: 8 lines covering the city. Runs 5 AM to midnight (24 hours on Saturdays). Clean and efficient.
- **Walking**: Barcelona is very walkable. The grid of Eixample makes navigation easy.
- **Bicing**: City bike share for residents. Tourists can use Donkey Republic or similar apps.
- **Airport**: El Prat is 30 minutes by Aerobus or Metro L9. Aerobus drops you at Placa Catalunya.
- **Pro tip**: Buy tickets to Sagrada Familia and Park Guell online weeks ahead. They sell out.
- **Beware pickpockets**: La Rambla, Metro, and crowded beaches. Front pockets, zipped bags.

## Useful Catalan/Spanish phrases

| English | Catalan/Spanish | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Hola | OH-la |
| Thank you | Gracies / Gracias | GRAH-see-es / GRAH-see-as |
| Please | Si us plau / Por favor | see oos PLOW / por fa-VOR |
| The bill please | El compte, si us plau / La cuenta | el COMP-teh see oos PLOW |
| Cheers! | Salut! / Salud! | sa-LOOT |
| Delicious! | Molt bo! / Muy rico! | mol BOH / mwee REE-ko |
| Where is...? | On es...? / Donde esta...? | on ES / DON-deh es-TAH |
| Good morning | Bon dia / Buenos dias | bon DEE-a / BWEH-nos DEE-as |
